options.text: This solution can be used to create a custom image for Azure Virtual Desktop which will then be distributed to a compute gallery of your choosing.

elements.name: deploymentPrefix
elements.label: Deployment Prefix
elements.toolTip: Optionally, provide a prefix (from 2 to 6 characters) for the deployments created by this template.

constraints.validationMessage: The prefix must be between 2 and 6 characters

options.text: The Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) provides a recommended resource naming convention which specifies that the abbreviation for the resource type is listed first in the name. This solution automatically names resources using the CAF naming convention and suggested resource type abbreviations. Some customers have chosen to move the resource type to the end of the resource names. Select 'Yes' below to ensure that resources are named with this reverse convention or leave it set to 'No' to follow the CAF.

elements.name: nameConvResTypeAtEnd
elements.label: Move the Resource Type to the end of resource names

options.text: Prior to deployment, make sure you meet the required prerequisites outlined in the required prerequisites section of the Getting Started documentation.

This solution fully supports the Zero Trust approach to security. If you wish to follow this approach, you must keep all your artifacts (i.e., scripts, installers, etc.) on an internal trusted storage location such as Azure Blob Storage. In addition, the storage account must have private endpoints configured and only be accessible through a user assigned identity.

The repo containing this solution, includes an automated script and supporting artifacts to configure the storage account, blob container, managed identity, and upload the required artifacts to the storage account.

Select the check box below to enable Zero Trust and select the storage account, blob container, and user assigned identity that has been assigned the 'Storage Blob Data Reader' role the storage account. The User Assigned Identity will be assigned the Contributor and Storage Blob Data Contributor roles on the Build Resource Group in order to support logging and deployment cleanup.

elements.name: storageAccount
elements.label: Image Artifacts Storage Account
elements.toolTip: Select the storage account containing the image customization artifacts (i.e., installers, scripts).

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('prereqs').storage.storageAccountsApi.value, (sa) => parse(concat('{"label":"', sa.name, '","description":"Location: ', sa.location, ' Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(sa.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"id":"', sa.id, '","blobEndpoint":"', sa.properties.primaryEndpoints.blob, '"}}')))]

elements.name: container
elements.label: Image Artifacts Container
elements.toolTip: Select the container which contains the software and script blobs.

options.text: To collect customization logs, select a storage account deployed by the imageManagement solution (with 'deployBuildLogsStorageAccount = true') and choose the container to write logs to. All standard and error output is written to individual blobs.

elements.name: collectLogs
elements.label: Collect customization logs in a storage container

elements.name: existingLogsStorageAccount
elements.label: Logs Storage Account
elements.toolTip: Select the storage account from the imageManagement resource group that will receive build logs. Deploy imageManagement with 'deployBuildLogsStorageAccount = true' to create one.

elements.name: logsContainer
elements.label: Logs Container
elements.toolTip: Select the blob container to write customization logs to.

elements.name: resourceGroup
elements.label: Resource Group (for Image Build resources)
elements.toolTip: Select the name of the existing resource group where the image build virtual machines and associated resources will be created.

options.text: When using an existing resource group, the User Assigned Identity selected below must already have the 'Contributor' role on that resource group. This deployment will NOT grant that role. Use the imageManagement deployment with 'Deploy Image Build Resource Group' enabled to pre-create the resource group and pre-grant the required role on the managed identity.

The image build resource group is TEMPORARY. A new uniquely-named resource group is created at the start of each build (timestamp suffix appended automatically) and is deleted along with all its contents when the build completes. Do not place resources you want to keep in this resource group.

elements.name: customBuildResourceGroupName
elements.label: Custom Build Resource Group Base Name
elements.subLabel: Optional. A deployment timestamp will be appended automatically to ensure uniqueness. Leave blank to use the default CAF-compliant name.
elements.toolTip: Optional base name for the temporary image build resource group. A timestamp suffix (yyyyMMddHHmmss) is always appended. The resource group is deleted automatically when the build completes.

constraints.validationMessage: If provided the value must be between 2 and 75 characters (a 14-character timestamp suffix will be appended).

options.text: Select a User Assigned Identity to attach to the build VMs. A UAI is required when using an existing resource group or when storage features (zero-trust artifacts or log collection) are enabled. This deployment grants no storage or resource group roles — all required permissions must be pre-granted: 'Virtual Machine Contributor' on the image build resource group (existing RG only), 'Storage Blob Data Reader' on the artifacts storage account (if zero-trust storage is enabled), and 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' on the logs storage account (if log collection is enabled). When creating a new resource group without storage features, the UAI is optional — one will be auto-created and granted VM Contributor. The managed identity output from the imageManagement deployment satisfies all requirements when configured correctly.

elements.name: userAssignedIdentity
elements.label: User Assigned Identity
elements.toolTip: Select an existing User Assigned Identity to attach to the build VMs. All required roles (VM Contributor on the build resource group, storage roles if applicable) must be pre-granted before running this deployment.

elements.name: source
elements.label: Image Source
elements.toolTip: Select the type of image to deploy on the session hosts.

elements.name: offer
elements.label: Offer
elements.toolTip: Select the desired marketplace image offer.

elements.name: sku
elements.label: SKU
elements.toolTip: Select the desired marketplace image SKU.

elements.name: imageDefinition
elements.label: Image Definition
elements.toolTip: Select the desired image definition.

elements.name: downloadLatestMicrosoftContent
elements.label: Download Microsoft Sources from Web
elements.toolTip: Select 'Yes' to download the latest sources for FSLogix, M365 Deployment Tool, OneDrive, and Teams from the Web.

options.text: This page of the wizard allows you to add image customizations by downloading blobs from the storage account selected on the 'Prerequisites' page and performing the defined actions. The blobs for the predefined installers listed below (i.e., FSLogix, Office, OneDrive, Teams, Virtual Desktop Optimization Tool) must be present in the storage account. The easiest way to upload them to the storage account is through the Deploy-ImageManagementResources script.

This page of the wizard allows you to add predefined and custom defined image customizations. The built-in customizations (i.e., FSLogix, Office, OneDrive, Teams, Windows Desktop Optimization Tool) require Internet access in order to be able to download the installers from Microsoft. If this is not available, then you must enable the Zero Trust option after completing the prerequisite deployment of Image Management Resources.

This page of the wizard allows you to add predefined and custom defined image customizations. The FSLogix, M365 Apps, OneDrive, and Teams installations require access to the air-gapped cloud-specific download locations in order to be able to download the installers required. If this is not available, then you must enable the Zero Trust option after completing the prerequisite deployment of Image Management Resources.

elements.name: installFsLogix
elements.label: Install FsLogix Agent
elements.toolTip: Check to install FsLogix Agent

elements.name: office365AppsToInstall
elements.label: Office 365 ProPlus Apps
elements.toolTip: Select the list of Office 365 ProPlus apps you wish to install.

elements.name: installOneDrive
elements.label: Install OneDrive (Per Machine)
elements.toolTip: Select to install OneDrive in per-machine mode for VDI

elements.name: installTeams
elements.label: Install Teams for VDI
elements.toolTip: Select to install the New Teams client for VDI.

options.text: Teams will not be installed with the media optimizations enabled as the required installers are not available on Air-Gapped clouds. In order to support optimizations, you should download Webview2, the WebRTC Redirector Service, and the latest Visual Studio C++ Redistributables, and put them in a storage account and then set up the zero trust configuration with a user-assigned managed identity.

elements.name: appsToRemove
elements.label: Built-in Apps to Remove
elements.toolTip: Select the built-in AppX packages you wish to remove.

elements.name: applyWindowsDesktopOptimizations
elements.label: Apply Windows Desktop Optimizations
elements.toolTip: Select to apply the customizations prescribed in the Windows Desktop Optimization Tool

elements.name: cleanupDesktop
elements.label: Cleanup the Common Desktop
elements.toolTip: Select to remove all items from the common desktop

elements.name: addCustomSoftware
elements.label: Add Custom Software
elements.toolTip: Select to add custom software to the image

options.text: The list of customizations and software in this section must be installed silently without user interaction. In addition they must be able to survive restarts, sysprep, and not generate system specific identifier as the image will be used on multiple session host virtual machines. Use the VDI Customizations section below to install software that can be installed in a way not to generate a system specific identifier until after a system restart.

Enter customizations for your image below. The columns are explained as follows: 1. Name: Enter the name of the customization. Can only contain alphanumerics and no spaces,2.  Blob Name (or URI): Enter the case-sensitive blob name or url, optionally, 3. Arguments: Enter any arguments, and finally, Restart: Optionally, select Yes or No to introduce a system restart after this customizer runs.

validations.message: Only alphanumerics and dashes (-) are allowed up to 24 characters

elements.name: addVDICustomSoftware
elements.label: Add VDI Custom Software
elements.toolTip: Select to add custom software to the image

options.text: The list of customizations and software in this section must be installed silently without user interaction. In addition, they must be able to survive sysprep. Installing software using this parameter allows the installation of agents and other software items that can be configured to generate a unique system identifier after a restart.

Enter customizations for your image below. The columns are explained as follows: 1. Name: Enter the name of the customization (can only contain alphanumerics and no spaces), 2. Blob Name (or url): enter the case-sensitive blob name or url, optionally 3. Arguments: any arguments.

validations.message: Only alphanumerics and dashes (-) are allowed up to 24 characters

elements.name: installUpdates
elements.label: Install Windows Updates
elements.toolTip: Select to install updates and specify an update source.

elements.name: updateUwpApps
elements.label: Update Built-In UWP (Store) Apps
elements.toolTip: Select to trigger a Microsoft Store update scan and update all built-in UWP apps provisioned in the image. Note: this option requires access to the Microsoft Store and may not work in air-gapped or restricted network environments.

options.text: Updating built-in UWP apps requires outbound access to the Microsoft Store. This option may not work in air-gapped or restricted network environments (e.g., IL5/Top Secret clouds). If the update scan fails, the step will log a warning and continue without failing the build.

elements.name: updateService
elements.label: Software update service
elements.toolTip: Select the appropriate update source for your environment.

elements.name: wsusServer
elements.label: WSUS Url
elements.toolTip: Specify an accessible WSUS server. Include the port if necessary.

options.text: Disable Update Channels: Select which software update channels to permanently disable in the image. Each selected channel will have its update service, scheduled tasks, and policy keys configured to prevent automatic updates after deployment. Recommended for pooled host pools where VMs are replaced on a regular cadence rather than patched in place.

elements.name: disableSoftwareUpdates
elements.label: Disable Update Channels
elements.toolTip: Select one or more update channels to disable in the image. Leave empty to keep all channels enabled.

allowedValues.label: Windows Update
allowedValues.description: Disables the Windows Update and Update Orchestrator services and sets AU policy to never check.

allowedValues.label: Microsoft 365 / Office
allowedValues.description: Disables Click-to-Run auto-updates and hides the update controls in Office apps.

allowedValues.label: Microsoft Teams
allowedValues.description: Disables auto-update for both new Teams and the classic machine-wide installer.

allowedValues.label: OneDrive
allowedValues.description: Prevents the OneDrive per-machine updater from running.

allowedValues.label: Microsoft Edge
allowedValues.description: Disables the Edge update service, edgeupdatem service, and scheduled update tasks.

allowedValues.label: WebView2 Runtime
allowedValues.description: Prevents the WebView2 runtime from self-updating via the Edge update channel.

allowedValues.label: Microsoft Store / UWP Auto-Update
allowedValues.description: Disables the Store update scan pipeline (AutoDownload policy and InstallService scheduled tasks) and prevents pre-installed UWP apps from silently updating via cloud content delivery.

options.text: For image management, this solution will create a new image version of a image (definition) in the Azure Compute Gallery selected on the Basics tab. You have the option to use an existing image definiton or define a new one.

link.label: Learn more about managing images with an Azure Compute Gallery.

elements.name: useExImageDef
elements.label: Use Existing Image Definition
elements.toolTip: Determines if you want to use an existing image definition from the Azure Compute Gallery selected on the 'Prerequisites' page or create a new one.

transforms.minimized: value[*].{name: name, id: id, securityType: properties.features[?name=='SecurityType'].value | [0], isAcceleratedNetworkSupported: properties.features[?name=='IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported'].value | [0], diskControllerTypes: properties.features[?name=='DiskControllerTypes' || name=='diskControllerTypes'].value | [0], isHibernateSupported: properties.features[?name=='IsHibernateSupported'].value | [0]}

elements.name: existingImageDefinition
elements.label: Image Definition
elements.toolTip: Select the existing image definition for which a new image version will be selected. If the desired image definition is not available, check your Compute Gallery selection on the 'Prerequisites' page or select to create a new one above.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.imagesApi.transformed.minimized, (imageDef) => parse(concat('{"label":"', imageDef.name, '","description":"Accelerated Networkingt: ', if(empty(imageDef.isAcceleratedNetworkSupported), 'No', 'Yes'), ' | NVMe: ', if(contains(string(if(empty(imageDef.diskControllerTypes), '', imageDef.diskControllerTypes)), 'NVMe'), 'Yes', 'No'), ' | SecurityType: ', if(empty(imageDef.securityType), 'Standard', imageDef.securityType), '","value":{"id":"', imageDef.id, '","IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported":', if(equals(imageDef.isAcceleratedNetworkSupported, 'True'), 'true', 'false'), ',"DiskControllerTypes":"', if(empty(imageDef.diskControllerTypes), 'SCSI', imageDef.diskControllerTypes), '","SecurityType":"', if(empty(imageDef.securityType), 'Standard', imageDef.securityType), '","IsHibernateSupported":', if(equals(imageDef.isHibernateSupported, 'True'), 'true', 'false'), '}}')))]

options.text: The selected image definition has incompatible feature combinations. Confidential VMs cannot be combined with Hibernation, Accelerated Networking, or NVMe. Hibernation cannot be combined with NVMe. Please select a different image definition or create a new one.

elements.name: existingImageDefRegionalIncompatWarning
elements.visible: [and(equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.useExImageDef, true), not(or(and(contains(coalesce(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.SecurityType, 'Standard'), 'Confidential'), or(equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.IsHibernateSupported, true), equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported, true), contains(coalesce(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.DiskControllerTypes, ''), 'NVMe'))), and(equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.IsHibernateSupported, true), contains(coalesce(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.DiskControllerTypes, ''), 'NVMe')))), or(and(contains(coalesce(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.SecurityType, 'Standard'), 'Confidential'), equals(length(steps('basics').resourceSkusApi.transformed.confidentialVMSizes), 0)), and(equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.IsHibernateSupported, true), equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported, true), equals(length(steps('basics').resourceSkusApi.transformed.hibernationAccelNetVMSizes), 0))))]

options.text: The selected image definition requires features that have no compatible VM sizes available in this region. Please select a different region, choose a different image definition, or create a new one with compatible features.

Use the first text box below to provide a custom name for the image definition, if desired. If you leave the custom name blank, then the image definition name will be created based on the 'publisher', 'offer', and 'sku' provided below using CAF naming standards.

elements.name: customImageDefinitionName
elements.label: Custom Image Definition Name
elements.toolTip: The name of the image definition to create in the Image Gallery. Leave blank if you want to allow this deployment to create an image definition in the Image Gallery chosen on the 'Prerequisites' page with a name based on the selections below.

constraints.validationMessage: When provided, the name must be between 2 and 80 characters long and only use valid characters.

elements.name: imageDefinitionPublisher
elements.label: Image Definition Publisher
elements.toolTip: The compute gallery image definition Publisher.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must contain only valid characters, no spaces, and be between 2 and 128 characters in length.

elements.name: imageDefinitionOffer
elements.label: Image Definition Offer
elements.toolTip: The compute gallery image definition Offer.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must contain only valid characters, no spaces, and be between 2 and 64 characters in length.

elements.name: imageDefinitionSku
elements.label: Image Definition Sku
elements.toolTip: The compute gallery image definition Sku.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must contain only valid characters, no spaces, and be between 2 and 64 characters in length.

elements.name: securityType
elements.label: Security type
elements.toolTip: Choose a type of security that matches your needs: Standard includes basic protections at no additional cost. Trusted launch virtual machines provide additional security features on Gen2 virtual machines to protect against persistent and advanced attacks. Confidential VM options are unavailable when Hibernation, NVMe, or Accelerated Networking features are enabled.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(or(equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.isHibernateSupported, true), equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.isNVMeSupported, true), equals(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.isAcceleratedNetworkSupported, true)), parse('[{"label":"Standard","value":"Standard"},{"label":"Trusted Launch","value":"TrustedLaunch"},{"label":"Trusted Launch Supported","value":"TrustedLaunchSupported"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Standard","value":"Standard"},{"label":"Trusted Launch","value":"TrustedLaunch"},{"label":"Trusted Launch Supported","value":"TrustedLaunchSupported"},{"label":"Confidential VM","value":"ConfidentialVM"},{"label":"Confidential VM Supported","value":"ConfidentialVMSupported"},{"label":"Trusted Launch and Confidential VM Supported","value":"TrustedLaunchAndConfidentialVmSupported"}]'))]

options.text: Confidential VMs do NOT support Accelerated Networking, NVMe storage, or hibernation. Only specific DC-series, EC-series, and NCC-series VM sizes support confidential computing. For more information, see the Azure Confidential Computing FAQ.

elements.name: isAcceleratedNetworkSupported
elements.label: Accelerated Networking Supported
elements.toolTip: Set to 'Yes' to allow Virtual Machines deployed from the image to use Accelerated Networking. When set to 'Yes' on the Image definition, capturing VMs that don't support accelerated networking is not supported.

elements.name: isHibernateSupported
elements.label: Hibernation Supported (preview)
elements.toolTip: Set this to 'Yes' to enable Virtual Machines to be deployed from this image definition that support hibernation. Note: In some regions, no VM sizes support both Hibernation and Accelerated Networking. NOT compatible with Confidential VMs or NVMe.

options.text: Hibernation is only supported on specific VM sizes (B-series v2, D/E-series v4/v5 with up to 64GB RAM, and NV-series v4/v5 with up to 112GB RAM). Hibernation does NOT support NVMe disks or Confidential VMs. VM sizes with NVMe storage (v6 and newer generations) are incompatible with hibernation. See the hibernation and NVMe documentation for complete details.
No VM sizes are available that support both Hibernation and Accelerated Networking in this region. Please disable one of these features to continue.

elements.name: isNVMeSupported
elements.label: NVMe Storage Support
elements.toolTip: Set this to 'Yes' to enable the creation of an image that supports VMs using NVMe storage (v6 and newer generation VMs). NVMe does NOT support hibernation or Confidential VMs.

elements.name: useCustomVersion
elements.label: Specify Custom Version Information
elements.toolTip: Select 'Yes' to specify the Major.Minor.Patch version information or 'No' to generate the Image Version based on the Date and Time (i.e., yyyy.MMdd.mmss).

elements.name: imageMajorVersion
elements.label: Image Major Version
elements.toolTip: Pick the first component of the image version

elements.name: imageMinorVersion
elements.label: Image Minor Version
elements.toolTip: Pick the second component of the image version

elements.name: imagePatch
elements.label: Image Patch
elements.toolTip: Pick the last component of the image version

elements.name: eolDaysFromNow
elements.label: End of Life from today in days
elements.toolTip: Enter the number of days before this image version should be replaced.

elements.name: storageAccountType
elements.label: Storage Account Type
elements.toolTip: Select the storage account type for this image version for the region where it is being captured.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(empty(filter(first(map(filter(steps('basics').storageApi.value, (resTypes) => equals(resTypes.resourceType, 'storageAccounts')), (sa) => sa.zoneMappings)), (zm) => equals(zm.location, steps('basics').scope.location.displayName))), parse('[{"label":"Standard LRS","value":"Standard_LRS"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Standard LRS","value":"Standard_LRS"},{"label":"Standard ZRS","value":"Standard_ZRS"}]'))]

elements.name: replicaCount
elements.label: Replica Count
elements.toolTip: Specify the default replica count per region for this image version. If you don't specify additional replication regions, then this value will be used with the deployment location.

elements.name: excludeFromLatest
elements.label: Exclude from Latest
elements.toolTip: Select the value for Exclude from Latest which determines whether this image version will be used when a image resource id uses 'latest' for the version. If you don't specify additional replication regions, then this value will be used with the deployment location.

elements.name: specifyReplicationRegions
elements.label: Specify more than one location where the image version should be stored?

options.text: Use the grid below to add All replication regions and the associated values. To add rows, toggle one of the drop down boxes.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(empty(filter(first(map(filter(steps('basics').storageApi.value, (resTypes) => equals(resTypes.resourceType, 'storageAccounts')), (sa) => sa.zoneMappings)), (zm) => equals(toLower(replace(zm.location, ' ', '')), last(take(steps('imageDest').imageVersion.replicationRegions, $rowIndex)).name))), parse('[{"label":"Standard LRS","value":"Standard_LRS"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Standard LRS","value":"Standard_LRS"},{"label":"Standard ZRS","value":"Standard_ZRS"}]'))]

options.text: To recover from a regional outage, it is recommended to maintain an Azure Compute Gallery in a secondary region, as the Azure Compute Gallery is not a global resource. By selecting 'Yes' below, you will be able to choose an Azure Compute Gallery in a secondary region, and a matching image definition and image version will automatically be created in that region.

elements.name: specifyRemoteGallery
elements.label: Specify a remote Azure Compute Gallery for regional disaster recovery purposes.

elements.name: remoteComputeGallery
elements.label: Remote Compute Gallery
elements.toolTip: Select a Azure Compute Gallery that is not in the same region as the gallery chosen on the 'prereqs' screen.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('imageDest').disasterRecovery.computeGalleryApi.value, (cg) => not(equals(cg.location, steps('prereqs').gallery.gallery.location))), (cg) => parse(concat('{"label":"', cg.name, '","description":"Location: ', cg.location, '","value":{"id":"', cg.id, '","location":"', cg.location, '"}}')))]

elements.name: storageAccountType
elements.label: Storage Account Type
elements.toolTip: Select the storage account type for this image version for the region where it is being captured.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(empty(filter(first(map(filter(steps('basics').storageApi.value, (resTypes) => equals(resTypes.resourceType, 'storageAccounts')), (sa) => sa.zoneMappings)), (zm) => equals(toLower(replace(zm.location, ' ', '')), steps('imageDest').disasterRecovery.remoteComputeGallery.location))), parse('[{"label":"Standard LRS","value":"Standard_LRS"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Standard LRS","value":"Standard_LRS"},{"label":"Standard ZRS","value":"Standard_ZRS"}]'))]

elements.name: replicaCount
elements.label: Replica Count
elements.toolTip: Specify the default replica count per region for this image version. If you don't specify additional replication regions, then this value will be used with the deployment location.

elements.name: excludeFromLatest
elements.label: Exclude from Latest
elements.toolTip: Select the value for Exclude from Latest which determines whether this image version will be used when a image resource id uses 'latest' for the version. If you don't specify additional replication regions, then this value will be used with the deployment location.

options.text: Disk Encryption Sets are applied to both build VM OS disks and gallery image version replicas, keeping builds compliant with policies that require a DES on all VMs. The standard DES is applied to the orchestration VM and the image VM for non-Confidential VM builds. For Confidential VM builds, the Confidential VM DES is applied to the image VM OS disk instead; the standard DES is used as a fallback when no Confidential VM DES is provided.

elements.name: enableCmk
elements.label: Enable Customer Managed Key encryption
elements.toolTip: When enabled, a standard Customer Managed Key Disk Encryption Set must be selected. Applied to the orchestration VM OS disk, the image VM OS disk for non-Confidential VM builds (or as a fallback for Confidential VM builds), and gallery image version replicas.

elements.name: existingDiskEncryptionSetResourceId
elements.label: Standard Disk Encryption Set
elements.placeholder: Select a standard Disk Encryption Set...
elements.toolTip: Required. Select the standard DES created by the imageManagement template. Only DES of type Customer Managed Key or Platform + Customer Managed Keys are shown; Confidential VM DES are excluded. Applied to the orchestration VM OS disk, the image VM OS disk for non-Confidential VM builds (or as a fallback for Confidential VM builds), and gallery image version replicas.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('imageDest').encryption.diskEncryptionSetsApi.value, (des) => and(equals(des.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name), and(equals(toLower(first(skip(split(des.id, '/'), 4))), toLower(first(skip(split(steps('prereqs').gallery.gallery.id, '/'), 4)))), not(equals(des.properties.encryptionType, 'ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey'))))), (des) => parse(concat('{"label":"', des.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(des.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', des.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: confidentialVMEncryptionType
elements.label: Confidential VM Guest State Encryption
elements.toolTip: Specifies how the Confidential VM guest state is encrypted in gallery image version replicas. 'Encrypted with CMK' requires a Confidential VM DES created by the imageManagement template. Pass the imageManagement output 'confidentialVmDiskEncryptionSetResourceId' to the picker below.

allowedValues.label: Encrypted VM Guest State Only with PMK
Encrypted with CMK (requires Confidential VM DES)

elements.name: existingConfidentialVMDiskEncryptionSetResourceId
elements.label: Confidential VM Disk Encryption Set
elements.placeholder: Select a Confidential VM Disk Encryption Set...
elements.toolTip: Required. Only DES of type ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey are shown. This DES must have CVM Orchestrator key release permissions pre-configured. Applied to the image VM OS disk during Confidential VM builds and gallery image version guest state encryption in each replication region. Use the imageManagement template to create this.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('imageDest').encryption.diskEncryptionSetsApi.value, (des) => and(equals(des.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name), and(equals(toLower(first(skip(split(des.id, '/'), 4))), toLower(first(skip(split(steps('prereqs').gallery.gallery.id, '/'), 4)))), equals(des.properties.encryptionType, 'ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey')))), (des) => parse(concat('{"label":"', des.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(des.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', des.id, '"}')))]

options.text: Available VM sizes are filtered based on your image definition features selected on the 'Image Distribution Options' page. Feature Limitations: Confidential VMs (DC/EC/NCC series) do NOT support Accelerated Networking, NVMe, or hibernation. Hibernation does NOT support NVMe disks and is only supported on B/D/E/NV-series v4/v5 with specific size limits. NVMe requires v6 or newer generation VMs. Incompatible VM sizes are automatically excluded from the list below.

elements.name: vmSize
elements.label: Virtual Machine Size
elements.toolTip: Select the size of the virtual machines. Multi-session hosts should have 4 - 24 vCPUs. Single session host should have 2 or more vCPUs. Available sizes are dynamically filtered based on selected image definition features.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(if(coalesce(if(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.useExImageDef, contains(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.SecurityType, 'Confidential'), contains(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.securityType, 'Confidential')), false), steps('basics').resourceSkusApi.transformed.confidentialVMSizes, if(and(coalesce(if(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.useExImageDef, steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.IsHibernateSupported, steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.isHibernateSupported), false), coalesce(if(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.useExImageDef, steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported, steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.isAcceleratedNetworkSupported), false)), steps('basics').resourceSkusApi.transformed.hibernationAccelNetVMSizes, if(and(coalesce(if(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.useExImageDef, contains(coalesce(steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.existingImageDefinition.DiskControllerTypes, ''), 'NVMe'), steps('imageDest').imageDefinition.isNVMeSupported), false), elements.name: diskSizeGB
elements.label: OS Disk Size (GB)
elements.toolTip: Select the OS disk size for the image build VM. Choose a larger size when building images that require more disk space. 128 GB uses the image default.

elements.name: encryptionAtHost
elements.label: EncryptionAtHost
elements.toolTip: Check to enable 'EncryptionAtHost' for Disk Encryption.

options.text: Select the Virtual Network and Subnet to which the image build virtual machine will be attached. The Virtual Network options listed below are based on the region selected under 'Image Build Location' on the 'Deployment Basics' page. If you don't find the desired virtual network, change the region on the 'Basics' page.

Virtual machines must be able to resolve the blob private dns zone and route to the storage account endpoint for the storage account(s) selected on the Prereqs and Management Resources screen. Select the appropriate subnet that meets these requirements.

elements.name: subnet
elements.label: Image Capture Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for the image capture.
